name: kv_cache_management_v1
description: KV cache management training data for adapterOS inference pipeline. Covers per-request cache allocation with Metal buffer layout, LRU eviction under VRAM pressure, prefix caching with BLAKE3-keyed entries and single-flight deduplication, per-tenant quota enforcement with reservation-based tracking, and deterministic cache behavior for replay correctness. All examples use concrete memory calculations for realistic model architectures (1B-70B parameters, fp16/fp32).

entries.notes: 2500 examples across 5 subcategories: cache_allocation (500), cache_eviction (500), prefix_caching (500), quota_enforcement (500), cache_determinism (500)

provenance.review_notes: Synthetic dataset covering KV cache lifecycle from allocation through eviction, prefix sharing, tenant quota enforcement, and deterministic replay. Memory calculations use real transformer architectures with Metal buffer layouts. Slab sizes match adapterOS implementation: 128, 256, 512 token rounded allocations.

evaluation_gates: Cache allocation correctly computes K+V buffer sizes as 2 * seq_len * bytes_per_token
Slab rounding applies for sequences <= 512 tokens (128, 256, 512 boundaries)
LRU eviction selects lowest-priority then oldest entries first
Prefix cache keys are deterministic BLAKE3 hashes of (context_digest, tokens, tokenizer_hash, model_identity)
Single-flight deduplication prevents redundant prefill computation for concurrent misses
Per-tenant quota enforcement uses reservation-based tracking with 5-minute timeout
QuotaExceeded error returned when used + reserved + requested > quota_bytes
Cache coherence resets all entries when adapter stack generation changes
Deterministic tie-breaking uses lexicographic key ordering (BTreeMap iteration order)
Logical ticks (not wall-clock time) govern LRU ordering for replay correctness
intent: Train the model to reason precisely about KV cache memory management in multi-tenant transformer inference, including correct byte-level calculations, eviction policy decisions, prefix sharing economics, quota enforcement mechanics, and deterministic replay constraints specific to adapterOS.

cache_allocation.description: Per-request KV cache allocation: Metal buffer layout, slab rounding, bytes_per_token calculation for various model architectures, K+V offset tracking, capacity checks, OOM handling

cache_eviction.description: VRAM pressure eviction: LRU policy, priority-based eviction (tenant tier, request age), cascading eviction, free region coalescing, coherence reset on adapter hot-swap

prefix_caching.description: Shared prefix KV reuse: BLAKE3-keyed entries, hit/miss patterns, longest-prefix matching, invalidation on prefix change, memory savings calculation, single-flight deduplication, integrity validation

quota_enforcement.description: Per-tenant cache quotas: reservation-based tracking, check/reserve/finalize/rollback lifecycle, quota exceeded handling, unlimited vs bounded tenants, eviction counter tracking, concurrent quota updates

cache_determinism.description: Deterministic cache behavior: logical tick ordering, generation tracking, coherence guarantee on adapter swap, BTreeMap iteration determinism, replay-safe eviction, receipt-bound cache attestation
